@@ -184,7 +184,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2a2.5","title":"Reverse-Round: E2E tests - cross-round integration and branch-diversity verification","description":"E2E tests: verify multiple rounds compose correctly (e.g., allocator round R8 interacts with loader round R7 without violating safety lattice). Verify branch-diversity constraint across round milestones. Regression tests ensuring no round mapping regresses when upstream math kernels change.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:07.660305734Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T23:25:59.293519118Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T23:25:59.293308370Z","close_reason":"Implemented reverse-round cross-round integration and milestone branch-diversity gate with structured logs/report, extended contract artifact coverage, and passing targeted harness tests. Verified via rch exec cargo test -p frankenlibc-harness --test reverse_round_contracts_test -- --nocapture and rch exec cargo check --workspace --all-targets. Workspace cargo fmt --check and cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings remain blocked by unrelated pre-existing worktree issues in crates/frankenlibc-abi/build.rs, crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/structured_log_test.rs, and crates/frankenlibc-membrane/tests/alien_cs_e2e_test.rs.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-test","frankenlibc","reverse-round","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2a2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2a2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2a2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2a2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2a2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-32e.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2a2.5","depends_on_id":"bd-5vr.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2a2.6","title":"Reverse-Round: Logging - round execution, math family selection, coverage metrics","description":"Logging spec for reverse-rounds: TRACE for per-subsystem math application detail, DEBUG for math family selection decisions and diversity constraint checks, INFO for round completion and coverage milestone events, WARN for diversity constraint near-violations (single family approaching 40% threshold), ERROR for constraint violations. All records include trace_id.

## Acceptance Criteria
See `acceptance_criteria` field.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
Implementation must preserve all existing functionality and claims while adding deterministic verification coverage.

### Mandatory Verification Gate
- Comprehensive unit tests are required for all touched logic: positive, negative, adversarial, and regression.
- Deterministic e2e scripts are required for all externally visible behavior in strict and hardened mode where relevant.
- Structured logs are mandatory for unit/e2e runs with fields: `trace_id`, `mode`, `api_family`, `symbol`, `decision_path`, `healing_action`, `errno`, `latency_ns`, `artifact_refs`.
- Closure evidence must include exact test commands, expected outputs/thresholds, and representative failure signatures.
- CI must persist logs, diffs, and reports with deterministic naming and retention for replay/triage.
- Tooling contract: deterministic orchestration/traceability uses `/dp/asupersync`, and deterministic diff/snapshot/TUI evidence uses `/dp/frankentui`.","notes":"### Mandatory Test/E2E Logging Checklist
- Test command set is documented in bead notes before closure (unit + e2e + perf where applicable).
- Expected output and pass/fail thresholds are documented per command.
- Failure triage snippets are documented (top failure classes + where to inspect artifacts).
- Evidence index entry links logs, parity diffs, and benchmark/proof artifacts.
- Overload behavior logging is required when applicable: pressure signal, degradation mode, selected policy, guard decision, rollback trigger.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MagentaFalcon","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:05:42.343421838Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T06:21:18.977440460Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T06:21:18.977370990Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["frankenlibc","logging","observability","reverse-round"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2a2.6","depends_on_id":"bd-2a2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2a2.6","depends_on_id":"bd-32e.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2a2.6","depends_on_id":"bd-5vr.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2a2.6","depends_on_id":"bd-oai.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":742,"issue_id":"bd-2a2.6","author":"MagentaFalcon","text":"Completed reverse-round logging coverage in `crates/frankenlibc-membrane/src/runtime_math/mod.rs` and validated via remote offload (`rch`).

Implemented reverse-round events and metrics:
- `runtime_reverse_round_math_selection` (`debug`): emits active-family count, total decisions, dominant family/share.
- `runtime_reverse_round_coverage_milestone` (`info`): emits milestone target/reached status.
- `runtime_reverse_round_diversity_near_violation` (`warn`) and `runtime_reverse_round_diversity_violation` (`error`): emitted from dominant-family share threshold checks.

Also ensured traceability fields (`trace_id`, mode/family/symbol/decision_path/healing/errno/latency/artifact refs) are present in export rows and extended unit assertions to require reverse-round events.

Validation evidence (all via `rch`):
1. `cargo test -p frankenlibc-membrane runtime_math_log_jsonl_export -- --nocapture` ✅
2. `cargo check -p frankenlibc-membrane --all-targets` ✅
3. `cargo clippy -p frankenlibc-membrane --all-targets -- -D warnings` ✅
4. `cargo fmt --check --package frankenlibc-membrane` ✅","created_at":"2026-02-25T06:21:14Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bd","title":"Isomorphism proof protocol: required checklist for each optimization lever","description":"Extreme optimization mapping.

Deliverables:
- Standard proof template: ordering, tie-breaking, FP behavior, RNG behavior, golden verification.
- Enforce completion in PR/harness metadata before merge.

Acceptance:
- No optimization closes without completed isomorphism proof artifact.
- Auditors can replay proof steps from stored commands.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","notes":"2026-02-11 closure (CrimsonCove): isomorphism_proof_test (7 pass) + gate script. Proof categories, templates, applicable modules all validated.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:12.074314078Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:52:42.521959Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:52:42.521959Z","close_reason":"Isomorphism proof protocol operational. 7 harness tests pass. check_isomorphism_proof.sh validates proof template, categories, and examples.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","perf","proof"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bd","depends_on_id":"bd-f7r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-2bhw6","title":"syscall: pidfd_open current pid with invalid flags should return EINVAL exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for pidfd_open(getpid(), 1). Add focused ABI regression coverage for the invalid-flags contract on a live pid.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberSignal","created_at":"2026-04-21T10:49:40.011107326Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T10:49:45.843328353Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-2bhw6","title":"syscall: pidfd_open current pid with invalid flags should return EINVAL exactly","description":"Host glibc returns EINVAL for pidfd_open(getpid(), 1). Add focused ABI regression coverage for the invalid-flags contract on a live pid.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberSignal","created_at":"2026-04-21T10:49:40.011107326Z","created_by":"AmberSignal","updated_at":"2026-04-21T10:51:45.196304870Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T10:51:45.194224328Z","close_reason":"Locked down host-parity pidfd_open(getpid(), 1) behavior: exact EINVAL contract with focused ABI regression coverage; verified locally and via rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2bu","title":"Replacement levels model: L0 Interpose, L1 Hardened Interpose, L2 Partial Replace, L3 Full Replace","description":"Critique mapping: #4 + bottom-line.

Deliverables:
- Define replacement maturity levels with objective criteria and prohibited claims at each level.
- Tie README wording and release tags to these levels.

Acceptance:
- Every release advertises exactly one level with machine-checkable evidence.
- Claim drift test fails if docs overstate current level.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","notes":"2026-02-11 progress (BrownCompass): implemented machine-checked replacement-level claim drift guard and refreshed maturity assessment counts. Changes: (1) updated tests/conformance/replacement_levels.json current_assessment/current_state to support_matrix counts (total=230, implemented=87, raw_syscall=83, callthrough=54, stub=6) and added release_tag_policy (tag_format + per-level suffixes + current_release_level/example); (2) added README section 'Declared Replacement Level (Machine-Checked)' with explicit current claim line (L0 — Interpose) and release tag format; (3) extended scripts/check_replacement_levels.sh with Check 6 to fail on README/release-tag drift (including exactly-one claim line); (4) extended crates/glibc-rs-harness/tests/replacement_levels_test.rs with claim_drift_guard_consistent_with_readme_and_release_policy. Validation: scripts/check_replacement_levels.sh (PASS), cargo test -p glibc-rs-harness --test replacement_levels_test -- --nocapture (9 passed), scripts/check_packaging.sh (PASS), cargo fmt --all (PASS).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrownCompass","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:48:10.380516782Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:39:11.179953847Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:39:11.179934470Z","close_reason":"Implemented replacement-level maturity claim model with README + release-tag drift guards; replacement-level gate and harness tests now enforce single current claim and policy alignment.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","docs","roadmap"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2bu","depends_on_id":"bd-30h","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2cn","title":"Kernel: Sobol low-discrepancy probe scheduler (implement generator)","description":"Implement a deterministic Sobol sequence generator for probe scheduling.

Goal:
- Improve coverage efficiency vs RNG sampling.
- Maintain determinism and reproducibility.

Acceptance criteria:
- sobol.rs (or integrated into design.rs) provides next_u32/next_f64-like API (fixed-point preferred).
- Unit tests for known initial vectors/directions.
- No allocations; constant-time.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"LavenderStone","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:33:20.007967122Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T19:03:13.090866882Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T19:03:13.090840343Z","close_reason":"Implemented sobol.rs: Gray-code Sobol low-discrepancy sequence generator with Joe-Kuo 2010 direction numbers (8 dims, compile-time const, O(1) per point). 18 tests, clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2cn","depends_on_id":"bd-gn9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-26T06:17:29Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-2cr","title":"E2E: LD_PRELOAD smoke harness (real programs; strict + hardened)","description":"Critique mapping: #1.

Goal: demonstrate this works with real programs, not toy unit tests.

Deliverables:
- A smoke runner (script or harness command) that runs a curated suite:
  - coreutils (ls/cat/echo/env)
  - a small C test binary from tests/integration
  - at least one non-trivial dynamic binary (python -c 'print(1)' or busybox)
- Runs in both modes:
  - GLIBC_RUST_MODE=strict
  - GLIBC_RUST_MODE=hardened

Acceptance:
- No crashes, deadlocks, or panics.
- Non-zero exit codes are reported with captured diagnostics bundle.

Evidence & Test Requirements:
- Add comprehensive unit tests (happy-path, negative, adversarial, and regression cases) for all changed logic.
- Add or update deterministic e2e scripts that exercise strict and hardened modes where relevant.
- Emit structured detailed logs for test/e2e runs (trace_id, mode, API/symbol, outcome, errno, timing, and artifact refs).
- Bead cannot close until test commands and log artifact locations are documented.","notes":"2026-02-11 closure (CrimsonCove): e2e_suite_test (5 pass) + e2e_suite.sh + ld_preload_smoke.sh. Tests actually build and run with LD_PRELOAD, produce JSONL structured logs, and validate artifact indices.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCove","owner":"CobaltCompass","created_at":"2026-02-11T02:37:26.765300147Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T16:55:14.335403Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T16:55:14.335403Z","close_reason":"E2E LD_PRELOAD smoke harness operational. 5 e2e_suite_test pass (including actual LD_PRELOAD execution). scripts/e2e_suite.sh and scripts/ld_preload_smoke.sh provide strict+hardened mode coverage.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["critique","e2e"],"comments":[{"id":743,"issue_id":"bd-2cr","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Progress update: added scripts/ld_preload_smoke.sh implementing strict+hardened LD_PRELOAD smoke runner with curated suite (coreutils ls/cat/echo/env, tests/integration/link_test.c compiled binary, python3 fallback for non-trivial dynamic binary). Added per-case timeout enforcement (TIMEOUT_SECONDS), deterministic artifact layout under target/ld_preload_smoke/<run_id>, and bounded diagnostics bundle on non-zero outcomes (bundle.meta, env.txt, proc_self_maps.txt, stdout/stderr, summary.txt).

Validation run: TIMEOUT_SECONDS=2 scripts/ld_preload_smoke.sh
- Result: 0 pass / 12 fail; all cases timed out in both strict and hardened modes.
- Artifacts: target/ld_preload_smoke/20260211T031753Z

Status: implementation complete for runner + diagnostics; behavior acceptance (no crashes/deadlocks/panics) currently failing and now reproducible with captured evidence.","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:19:00Z"},{"id":744,"issue_id":"bd-2cr","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Debug addendum: strace on representative failing case (hi) shows child process hanging in  during startup before command output, then killed by timeout (exit 124). Trace captured at /tmp/ld_preload_echo.trace. This narrows next fix target to startup/init synchronization path under LD_PRELOAD.","created_at":"2026-02-11T03:19:24Z"}]}
